title: AI Is Not Here To Replace Software Engineers (At Least Not Yet)
excerpt: I used to say 'I could vibe code that in minutes.' Then I did. Then I checked the security. Hallucinated packages, exposed credentials, wide-open backdoors — here's what the hype leaves out and why the 80/20 rule still matters.

title: The Secret Keys Everyone's Accidentally Pushing to GitHub
excerpt: One bad commit, one exposed API key, one very bad Tuesday. Here's how to stop it from happening.

title: My 200-Line Boilerplate Prompt (And Why It Matters)
excerpt: 'Build me a website' vs a structured PRD. The difference between a toy and a real product starts in the prompt.

title: Why LLM Hallucinations Are a Security Problem
excerpt: When your AI downloads a package that doesn't exist, that's not just wrong — it's a potential attack vector.
